title: Claudetop – htop for Claude Code sessions (see your AI spend in real-time)
summary: Claudetop introduces an innovative open-source monitoring tool, drawing parallels to the system utility 'htop', but specifically tailored for Claude Code sessions. Its core functionality revolves around providing real-time visibility into AI resource consumption and the corresponding financial expenditure. This utility offers developers and researchers immediate, granular insights into their AI spend, which is crucial for fostering more effective cost management and enabling performance optimization throughout the development and deployment phases of projects that utilize Claude's powerful large language models. By distilling complex usage metrics into an easily digestible and intuitive interface, Claudetop directly addresses a significant and growing need for enhanced transparency and control over operational costs within the rapidly expanding domain of generative AI applications. This tool is poised to become an essential asset for development teams aiming to efficiently optimize their resource utilization and financial outlay when interacting with advanced AI models, thereby promoting more sustainable AI development practices.

title: Show HN: GitAgent – An open standard that turns any Git repo into an AI agent
summary: GitAgent is introduced as an open standard designed to address the fragmentation in AI agent development by defining agents directly within Git repositories. This innovative approach allows any Git repo to function as an AI agent, leveraging three core files: `agent.yaml` for configuration, `SOUL.md` for personality and instructions, and `SKILL.md` for capabilities. This standardization ensures a portable agent definition compatible with various leading frameworks such as Claude Code, OpenAI Agents SDK, CrewAI, Google ADK, and LangChain, eliminating the need for extensive rewrites when switching platforms. A key advantage of GitAgent's native integration with Git is the inherent support for robust development practices, including comprehensive version control for agent behavior, branching for environment promotion, human-in-the-loop mechanisms via pull requests, an audit trail, and capabilities for agent forking and remixing, promoting collaborative and auditable AI agent development.

title: Show HN: Zap Code – AI code generator that teaches kids real HTML/CSS/JS
summary: Zap Code is an innovative AI-powered code generator designed to teach children aged 8-16 foundational web development skills using real HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. It aims to bridge the significant gap between introductory block-based coding platforms like Scratch and actual text-based programming. Users can describe desired applications in plain English, and the AI immediately generates the corresponding code, rendered live in a preview. The platform features three interaction modes: visual-only adjustments, a read-only code view with educational annotations, and a full code editing environment supported by AI autocomplete. Technologically, Zap Code utilizes a Next.js frontend, a Node.js backend, and a simplified Monaco editor, ensuring a user-friendly experience while maintaining robust functionality. A progressive complexity engine adapts features based on a child's evolving skill level, making the transition to advanced coding smoother and more engaging. This approach allows kids to interact with tangible programming output first, fostering a deeper understanding of web development concepts.

title: Claude March 2026 usage promotion
summary: Anthropic's Claude AI model is set to feature a "Spring Break Usage Promotion" in March 2026, as indicated by a support page article. This strategic initiative suggests an effort by Anthropic to boost user engagement and adoption of its large language model during a specific seasonal period. While the provided information is concise, such promotions typically involve incentives like temporary increases in usage allowances, discounted access to advanced features, or special benefits designed to encourage both new and existing users to interact more frequently with the Claude platform. This marketing approach is common within the technology sector, particularly for AI services, as companies seek to expand their user base, gather valuable usage data, and foster a stronger community around their offerings. The promotion underscores Anthropic's continuous efforts to enhance user experience and maintain competitive positioning within the rapidly evolving artificial intelligence landscape, likely aiming to demonstrate the capabilities and value proposition of the Claude model to a wider audience.

title: Show HN: KeyID – Free email and phone infrastructure for AI agents (MCP)
summary: KeyID introduces a novel free infrastructure specifically designed to equip AI agents with essential email and phone communication capabilities. This platform aims to bridge the gap between autonomous AI systems and conventional human-centric communication channels, enabling AI agents to interact more seamlessly with external entities and services. By providing robust tools for managing email correspondence and initiating phone calls, KeyID facilitates the development of more versatile and practical AI applications. This infrastructure is particularly beneficial for AI agents requiring direct interaction for tasks such as customer support, scheduling, data gathering, or automated task execution in real-world scenarios. The 'MCP' designation likely points to a comprehensive, multi-channel communication platform, positioning KeyID as a crucial component for enhancing the operational scope and real-world utility of AI agents. It addresses a critical need for standardized and accessible communication layers, fostering greater integration of AI into daily workflows and business processes without incurring additional costs for fundamental communication services.
